title: Support dotted references in bind
body:
What would you like added/supported?

dotted references in bind

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

`Verilator 5.037 devel rev v5.036-162-g51545b36c`

Can you attach an example that runs on other simulators?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained and self-checking so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs

```
git diff
diff --git a/test_regress/t/t_bind2.v b/test_regress/t/t_bind2.v
index 273e21b31..308da8537 100644
--- a/test_regress/t/t_bind2.v
+++ b/test_regress/t/t_bind2.v
@@ -32,22 +32,6 @@ module t (/*AUTOARG*/
                           // Inputs
                           .clk                  (clk));
 
-   //Binding i_targetmod to mycheck --instantiates i_mycheck inside i_targetmod
-   //param1 not over-riden (as mycheck)                 (=> 0x31)
-   //param2 explicitly bound to targetmod value (=> 0x22)
-   //param3 explicitly bound to top value                       (=> 0x13)
-   //p1 implictly bound (.*), takes value from targetmod        (=> 0x04)
-   //p2 explictly bound to targetmod                                            (=> 0x05)
-   //p3 explictly bound to top                                                          (=> 0x03)
-
-   // Alternative unsupported form is i_targetmod
-   bind targetmod mycheck
-     #(
-       .param2(param2),
-       .param3(param3)
-       )
-   i_mycheck (.p2(p2), .p3(p3), .*);
-
 endmodule
 
 module targetmod (input clk);
@@ -91,3 +75,14 @@ module mycheck (/*AUTOARG*/
       $finish;
    end
 endmodule
+
+   //Binding i_targetmod to mycheck --instantiates i_mycheck inside i_targetmod
+   //param1 not over-riden (as mycheck)                 (=> 0x31)
+   //param2 explicitly bound to targetmod value (=> 0x22)
+   //param3 explicitly bound to top value                       (=> 0x13)
+   //p1 implictly bound (.*), takes value from targetmod        (=> 0x04)
+   //p2 explictly bound to targetmod                                            (=> 0x05)
+   //p3 explictly bound to top                                                          (=> 0x03)
+
+   // Alternative unsupported form is i_targetmod
+   bind t.i_targetmod mycheck i_mycheck (.p2(p2), .p3(p3), .*);
```

```
%Error: t/t_bind2.v:88:10: syntax error, unexpected '.', expecting IDENTIFIER or IDENTIFIER-for-instance or ':' or randomize
   88 |    bind t.i_targetmod mycheck i_mycheck (.p2(p2), .p3(p3), .*);
      |          ^
```

**Compiles with VCS**
